description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array consisting of multiple blue photovoltaic modules arranged in a rectangular grid. The front surface is covered with a grid of blue solar cells, each separated by thin white lines, and framed by a metallic border. The array is supported by a black metal framework that elevates and tilts the panels at an angle, optimizing exposure to sunlight. The rear view reveals the structural support system, which includes angled braces and horizontal bars for stability. The overall construction appears robust, with the panels likely made of tempered glass and silicon cells, while the frame and supports are metallic. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with blue photovoltaic modules in a rectangular grid, supported by a black metal frame and tilted for optimal sunlight exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grid of blue solar panels with white grid lines and metallic framing.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the panels and the supporting black metal framework beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black metal support structure and the underside of the solar panels.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the tilt and support structure from the other side.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a black metal frame and angled steel supports
attribute_array_configuration: 4x4 grid of solar panels arranged in a rectangular array
